unresolved_core: ~150g (1/3 lb) medium soft cheese. Appenzeller works best. Gruyère would be my go-to alternative.

nonfood: Wood chips (optional, fuel for smokey flavour)

unresolved_core: 1 container of barbecue rub ([Traeger Pork and Poultry Rub](https://www.traeger.com/rubs-spices/pork-poultry) is recommended)
nonfood: Applewood (or other sweet cooking wood such as cherry or maple)

unresolved_core: 24 oz or 1 jar of Fradiavolo Sauce
500 g of pork ribs, cut into pieces
200 g of pork belly, cut into pieces
400g croutons or toasted bread in pieces
7 pears (Quality doesn't matter; you can argue that overripe pears are better because they have more sugar content.)
350 grams veal mince (if you cant find veal replace with pork)
2 slice cheese. Use a cheese that is mid-aged, not too savoury and not too delicate.
1 can (10-3/4 ounces) condensed cream of chicken soup, undiluted
2 lbs boneless chicken breast or thigh
225 g 50-70% dark chocolate chips or semi-sweet milk chocolate chips
1 large bunch collard greens chopped into ribbons
12 very thin slices of ham with Italian herbs
1 qt (32 oz, 946mL) **low-sodium** broth or stock (I prefer Pacific Foods)
4 slices of Canadian bacon or ham
10-12 oz kale, (escarole, swiss chard, or spinach can also be used).
400g Pizza dough (either bought or from [Pizza Dough](/pizza-dough))
2 big spoons of sour creme or creme fraiche
A whole ckicken weighing close to 2,5kg (roughly 3 pounds). Avoid industrially grown birds, as those can make the stew very greasy and flavorless.
300 ml Cassis-Juice (Johannesbeer), if you can't find it you can replace it with another juice, I did it e.g. with cranberry pomegranate juice
1 bottle 700ml red wine (I prefer Primitivo/Zinfandel), don't buy a cheap cooking wine, you want to drink it :)
1 spoon ginger powder (or a bit of fresh ginger)
1 cup dried fruit (apricots, unsweetened cranberries, dates, etc)
1 egg yolk whisked to brush cookies
2 cup assorted Lettuce, chopped or torn
1/2 cup pitted Black Olives (preferably brine-cured), coarsely chopped
One block of firm tofu (400g, extra firm will work too)
1/2 cup of tahini (also known as tehina)
1 (1.5 fluid ounce) jigger Irish cream liqueur
5 oregano sprigs / heavy sprinkle of oregano leaves
1 29oz large can tomato sauce (or 4 cups fresh tomato sauce)
2-3 chopped scallions white and green separated)
1 whole egg or 2 egg yolks, preferably at room temperature
1 lb beef tenderloin, or stew meat if you are frugal
2 large potatoes (Yukon Gold preferred, but any quality potatoes work)
400g risotto rice (Arborio, Carnaroli or Vialone Nano)
12 oz mushrooms (I reccommend shitake, but white, portabello, or maitake will also work.)
500g boiled sausage or boiled chicken meat
5 oz Boursin Garlic and Fine Herbs
1 (6 ounce) can frozen orange juice concentrate
500 g of rabbit, cut into pieces
1 (6 ounce) can frozen orange juice concentrate, thawed and undiluted
1 chilli (or 1 tsp of dried chilli flakes)
1 small can (around 14oz) of whole kernel corn
1 small can (around 14oz) of cream style corn
1.5kg meat (lamb or any other kind of meat)
420ml quark (plain yoghurt can be used as a replacement)
1-2 tortillas. Any brand works, but you can decide if you want the tortilla folded in half, or 2 tortillas together.
1 lb cubed stewing beef (chuck, short rib, or oxtail)
1-2 lb total of ground beef chuck, veal, and pork
500ml of milk (or if not using whey, 2l of milk)
Pasta noodles of choice (e.g. approximately 9oz of fettuccine noodles)
100g grain of choice (e.g. rye, spelt and wheat)
A large glass of red wine (approx. 250ml)
1kg chuck roast, cut in bite-sized pieces
450 g of lamb leg meat, diced
1 recipe of your favorite tuna salad
1 cup zaatar leaves (without the stalks), cleaned and dried.
